findings.message: The plan still leaves `ci.yml` `promote-app:*` guards unchanged, so app-scoped promotion PRs do not actually run lint/typecheck/test in `ci.yml`. That conflicts with the stated goal at line 38 that `ci.yml` is the sole validation workflow for all apps on dev/PR; if this carve-out is intentional, the goal/outcome language needs to say so explicitly.

The consumer-tracing claim is inaccurate. In the current `.github/workflows/merge-gate.yml`, `reception.yml` is only watched via `scopeWorkflowMap` for scoped promotions, not in the default PR path, so "merge-gate watches reception.yml completion" is not generally true.

The checkpoint assumption says "Reception main deploy works via wrangler deploy," but TASK-04 explicitly routes Reception through `apps/reception/scripts/worker-deploy-safe.mjs`, not raw `wrangler deploy`. That is an internal contract mismatch for the later checkpoint/replan step.

The TASK-06 row in the summary table still uses the older description ("Update merge-gate, auto-pr.yml, archive reusable-app.yml"), while the active-task list and TASK-06 body now also include trigger narrowing and path-classifier/filter cleanup.
